title: Oklahoma accuses State Farm of ‘corporate scheme’ to deny claims for disaster damage
description: “It is unacceptable that Oklahomans are paying rising homeowners insurance premiums yet receiving less protection in return,” Attorney General Gentner Drummond said.
content:
Oklahoma Attorney General Gentner Drummond has accused insurance giant State Farm of conducting a coordinated “corporate scheme” to deny homeowner claims for wind and hail damage.

de: Oklahoma wirft State Farm „Unternehmenspläne“ vor, um Ansprüche wegen Katastrophenschäden abzulehnen. „Es ist inakzeptabel, dass Oklahomaner steigende Prämien für die Hausbesitzerversicherung zahlen, aber im Gegenzug weniger Schutz erhalten“, sagte Generalstaatsanwalt Gentner Drummond.
en: Oklahoma accuses State Farm of ‘corporate scheme’ to deny claims for disaster damage. “It is unacceptable that Oklahomans are paying rising homeowners insurance premiums yet receiving less protection in return,” Attorney General Gentner Drummond said.

Am 29. April 2020 zog ein einzelner Blitz von Wolke zu Wolke in 7,39 Sekunden 768 Kilometer über Texas, Louisiana und Mississippi zurück – ein Blitz länger als die Fahrt von Boston nach Cleveland, die von der WMO als die längste jemals gemessene Strecke zertifiziert wurde.

On 29 April 2020, a single bolt of lightning stretched 768 kilometres across Texas, Louisiana, and Mississippi in one continuous flash, far enough to reach from New York City to Columbus, Ohio before the thunder from its first end had finished rolling. On 29 April 2020, a single cloud-to-cloud lightning bolt stretched 768 kilometres across Texas, Louisiana and Mississippi in 7.39 seconds — a flash longer than the drive from Boston to Cleveland, certified by the WMO as the longest ever measured at the time.

Die 29 mensis Aprilis anno 2020, una nubes ad nubem fulmen extendit 768 chiliometrorum trans Texas, Ludoviciana et Mississippi in 7.39 secundis — fulguri longior quam impulsio a Boston ad Cleveland, certior factus ab WMO ut longissimum umquam tempore mensuratum.
